question: Is the title text "Landscape Scroll: Turn the chaos into stillness, and give your heart to the clouds and wind" spelled correctly?
Is the title text displayed horizontally at the top of the scroll in an elegant cursive script?
Is the poem text "The moon shines bright upon the night, like frost upon the ground; I raise my head to see its light, then bow with thoughts homeward bound" spelled correctly?
Is the poem text displayed vertically on the right side of the painting?
Is the first maxim text "Seek not haste in reading, but remembrance; let words settle in your heart, let deeds rest in your hands" spelled correctly?
Is the second maxim text "Walk not for distance, but for clarity; let footsteps land in the present, let eyes gaze toward the horizon" spelled correctly?
Are both maxim texts displayed vertically on the left side of the scroll?
Is the annotation text "Note: This scroll uses only black ink and pale grey, proving gentleness needs no color to endure" spelled correctly?
Is the annotation text placed near the mountain base in a fine script style?
Is the bottom line text "Write as water flows, smooth and continuous, yet every word must remain legible and upright" spelled correctly?
Is the bottom line text rendered in clear print style at the bottom of the scroll?
Is the text "Still heart, slow steps, free as the wind" spelled correctly inside the bottom-right seal?
Is the seal at the bottom right square in shape and vermillion (red) in color?
Is the text "Let each day unfold with quiet grace" spelled correctly inside the bottom-left seal?
Is the seal at the bottom left rectangular in shape and vermillion (red) in color?
Is all calligraphy crisp and legible, appearing like high-definition print rather than sloppy?
Are all text characters free from garbled symbols, incomplete strokes, or unrecognizable glyphs?
Is the color palette strictly limited to black/grey ink and vermillion seals (no other colors)?
Do the text and seals look naturally integrated onto the rice paper texture (e.g., seals look authentically stamped)?
